name: Santolaya, Picayu, El Padrún, Río Nalón, Senda Verde Oviedo, Bosque de El Fulminato – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Oviède, located in Asturias, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features river valleys, such as those formed by the Nora and Nalón Rivers, and is framed by prominent mountain ranges like Monte Naranco and the Sierra del Aramo. These natural features provide varied terrain, from river-side paths to elevated mountain trails, suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Oviède The most popular mountain bike trail is Oviedo and Nalón River Loop, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route features the Senda Verde and El Furacón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Santolaya, Picayu, El Padrún, Río Nalón, Senda Verde Oviedo, Bosque de El Fulminato – ruta circular, a difficult 22.7 miles (36.6 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain including the Río Nalón and the Bosque de El Fulminato. Local mountain bikers also love the Church of San Miguel de Lillo – Saint Mary of Naranco loop from La Corredoria, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail leading through the Monte Naranco area, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Oviède is defined by river valleys, mountain ranges, and diverse geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes. The routes in Oviède are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 190 reviews. More than 1900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Oviède's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Oviedo?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Oviedo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from river valleys to mountain slopes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking near Oviedo?
A: Mountain biking near Oviedo offers a varied landscape. You'll find trails winding through river valleys, such as those formed by the Nora and Nalón Rivers, as well as more challenging routes on mountain ranges like Monte Naranco and the Sierra del Aramo. The region features a mix of natural features and geological formations.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Oviedo?
A: Yes, Oviedo offers trails suitable for families and beginners. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are 5 easy trails available. A good option for varying skill levels is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486029/oviedo-and-nalon-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oviedo and Nalón River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 21.4-mile (34.4 km) route that features the Senda Verde and El Furacón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Oviedo?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Oviedo are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486029/oviedo-and-nalon-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oviedo and Nalón River Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485642/santolaya-picayu-el-padrun-rio-nalon-senda-verde-oviedo-bosque-de-el-fulminato-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santolaya, Picayu, El Padrún, Río Nalón, Senda Verde Oviedo, Bosque de El Fulminato – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural attractions I can see along mountain bike trails in Oviedo?
A: Oviedo's trails often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You can explore areas like Monte Naranco, home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart of Jesus Monument&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2923226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Naranco&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the Nora and Nalón Rivers, and the Priañes Reservoir. For a unique natural experience, the Guanga or Buanga waterfalls are accessible via routes starting in San Andrés de Trubia.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the Guanga or Buanga waterfalls are a notable natural attraction in the region. You can access them via routes that typically start in San Andrés de Trubia, winding through native vegetation forests and offering stunning views of the Trubia Valley.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Oviedo?
A: Oviedo offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. The region's climate is generally mild, but spring and autumn often provide the most pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Oviedo?
A: The mountain biking experience in Oviedo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic river valleys, and the challenging mountain routes that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Oviedo offers 38 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31967253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Lillo – Saint Mary of Naranco loop from La Corredoria&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses the Monte Naranco area, provide significant elevation gain and technical sections. Other routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro Asturiano de Oviedo – Brañes Roman Bridge loop from Llamaquique&lt;/a&gt;, also offer a demanding ride.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trails in Oviedo using public transport?
A: Oviedo has a public transport network that can help you access various parts of the city and its surroundings. While specific trailheads might require a short ride or walk from a public transport stop, the city's central location makes it a good base for exploring the region without always needing a car. It's advisable to check local bus routes for access to specific starting points.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the region around Oviedo is known for its unique geological features. The Covarones of the Tuernes River, for example, showcase a remarkable karstic landscape with caves, stalactites, arches, and natural tunnels formed by river erosion. While some areas might require specific knowledge or equipment for exploration, the general landscape offers interesting geological sights.
tips: Many people think that the highest peak in Oviedo is Pico Paisano in the Sierra del Naranco, but it is Picayu or Picajo, also known as Escobín, in the Sierra de Fayéu, to the south of the council and bordering on Mieres. It can be climbed from several points.
The path crosses meadows, chestnut groves and riverside forests along the banks of the Nalón River.
